comments.text:
Hi Sean, could you take a look at this intermittent failure?

comments.author: sean.skim21@gmail.com
comments.raw_text:
I think I had this issue few times on Try when I worked on this patch (I believe that it had to do with the timing of [`MutationObserver`](https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/rev/a9824b3ffc2efea7a317f114bc3192ccdffa0ef7/netwerk/test/browser/browser_necko_l10n.js#29) when removing each expected messages), but it passed when I reran the test. 

Also it seems like more than [90% of the failures](https://treeherder.mozilla.org/intermittent-failures/bugdetails?startday=2024-09-19&endday=2025-01-17&tree=trunk&failurehash=all&bug=1928537) comes from Linux. I am wondering if it would be a good idea to disable the test on Linux.
comments.creator: sean.skim21@gmail.com

comments.author: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.raw_text:
Bug 1986649 changed the test to also check the statuspanellabel for the
expected string, however that value is not always synchronized with
the onStatusChange event, leading to intermittent failures.

This change fixes it so that we allow both "Looking up ..." and
"Transferring data from ..." status strings.
It is still possible that the test would be racy (other status panel
values are possible), but all current failures have one of these two strings.
comments.creator: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.text:
Created attachment 9536305
Bug 1928537 - Fix raciness in browser_necko_l10n.js r=#necko


Bug 1986649 changed the test to also check the statuspanellabel for the
expected string, however that value is not always synchronized with
the onStatusChange event, leading to intermittent failures.

This change fixes it so that we allow both "Looking up ..." and
"Transferring data from ..." status strings.
It is still possible that the test would be racy (other status panel
values are possible), but all current failures have one of these two strings.
